message:
fix: shorten mermaid node labels to prevent truncation in rendered diagrams (#5616)

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -158,35 +158,35 @@ The following diagram shows the full system architecture including all integrati ```mermaid graph TB - User["User
(terminal / VS Code / JetBrains / GitHub Action)"] - GitHub["GitHub OAuth"] - Backend["Console Backend :8080"] - Browser["User Browser"] - MCP["MCP Bridge
(kubestellar-ops MCP server +
kubestellar-deploy MCP server)"] - K8s["Kubernetes Clusters"] - Agent["kc-agent :8585"] - CLI["AI Coding Agent
(Claude Code, Copilot, Cursor,
Gemini CLI, etc.)"] - OpsStdio["kubestellar-ops
MCP server (stdio)"] - DeployStdio["kubestellar-deploy
MCP server (stdio)"] - AI["AI Provider API
(OpenAI-compatible endpoint:
Claude / OpenAI / Gemini)"] - - User -- "invokes" --> CLI - Browser -- "OAuth flow" --> Backend - Browser -- "authorize" --> GitHub - GitHub -- "access token" --> Backend - Backend -- "session JWT" --> Browser - Backend -- "REST / WebSocket" --> Browser - Browser -- "WebSocket" --> Agent - Backend -- "MCP/HTTP tool calls" --> MCP - MCP -- "Kubernetes API
(auth via kubeconfig)" --> K8s - Agent -- "kubectl
(auth via kubeconfig)" --> K8s - OpsStdio -- "Kubernetes API
(auth via kubeconfig)" --> K8s - DeployStdio -- "Kubernetes API
(auth via kubeconfig)" --> K8s - CLI -- "MCP (stdio)" --> OpsStdio - CLI -- "MCP (stdio)" --> DeployStdio - CLI -- "MCP protocol" --> Agent - Backend -- "AI chat completions" --> AI - CLI -- "AI prompts" --> AI + User["User"] + GitHub["GitHub OAuth"] + Backend["Console Backend\n:8080"] + Browser["Browser"] + MCP["MCP Bridge"] + K8s["Kubernetes\nClusters"] + Agent["kc-agent\n:8585"] + CLI["AI Coding Agent"] + OpsStdio["kubestellar-ops\nMCP (stdio)"] + DeployStdio["kubestellar-deploy\nMCP (stdio)"] + AI["AI Provider API"] + + User -- invokes --> CLI + Browser -- OAuth flow --> Backend + Browser -- authorize --> GitHub + GitHub -- access token --> Backend + Backend -- session JWT --> Browser + Backend -- REST / WS --> Browser + Browser -- WebSocket --> Agent + Backend -- MCP/HTTP --> MCP + MCP -- K8s API --> K8s + Agent -- kubectl --> K8s + OpsStdio -- K8s API --> K8s + DeployStdio -- K8s API --> K8s + CLI -- MCP stdio --> OpsStdio + CLI -- MCP stdio --> DeployStdio + CLI -- MCP protocol --> Agent + Backend -- AI completions --> AI + CLI -- AI prompts --> AI ``` KubeStellar Console supports two deployment modes with different security boundaries:
